channel.title: Cursor Blog
channel.description: Latest updates and insights from the Cursor team about AI-powered coding

item.title: Securing our codebase with autonomous agents
item.description: Cursor's security team built a fleet of security agents to find and fix vulnerabilities across a fast-changing codebase.

item.title: How we compare model quality in Cursor
item.description: We use a hybrid online-offline eval process to keep our understanding of model quality aligned with what developers actually do.

item.title: Over 30 new plugins join the Cursor Marketplace
item.description: Extend Cursor with prebuilt capabilities in our marketplace.

item.title: Build agents that run automatically
item.description: Cursor now supports automations that run based on triggers and instructions you define.

item.title: Cursor is now available in JetBrains IDEs
item.description: Use Cursor agents in IntelliJ IDEA, PyCharm, WebStorm, and other JetBrains IDEs through the Agent Client Protocol.

item.title: How technical support at Cursor uses Cursor
item.description: By collapsing code, logs, team knowledge, and past conversations into a single Cursor session, we've removed the context-gathering bottleneck for most of our support work.

item.title: PlanetScale protects production reliability with Bugbot
item.description: Bugbot saves PlanetScale the equivalent of two full-time engineers worth of review effort.

item.title: The third era of AI software development
item.description: A third era of AI software development is emerging as autonomous cloud agents take on larger tasks over longer timescales.

item.title: Closing the code review loop with Bugbot Autofix
item.description: Bugbot Autofix can find and automatically fix issues in PRs by spawning cloud agents to test your changes.

item.title: Cursor agents can now control their own computers
item.description: Cloud agents use the software they create to verify changes and demo their work.
